messages.text: Demolding , metal detector and c6 belts are running correctly
We are having problems with the Jaws at Cavanna; Maintenance is on the line.
The line stopped because maintenance is trying to fix the issue

Flow-wrap packaging check update 


The flow wrap is checked and looks good, with well-sealed edges and a good appearance. The lot number is legible and printed in the location required by the spec.

Tempering chart check update 


temperature remains stable—slightly high, but within the permissible range required by the Centerline.

Weight check update 


The weight looks good; we are within the parameters required by the product specifications.
The line is running,maintenance still checking the cavanna

Cavanna started outputting doubles. I ended up adding shims to all 3 blades. I also tightened down the outfeed belt and checked if the head was level. I have not noticed any more doubles being produced or any sealing issues.
Demolding , metal detector and c6 belts are running correctly

Weight check update 


weight looks good; we are within the parameters required by the product specifications.

Flow-wrap packaging check update 


The flow wrap is checked and looks good, with well-sealed edges and a good appearance. The lot number is legible and printed in the location required by the spec.
During cleaning, the hopper was checked with maintenance, and no issues were found.
This Truffle Line pallet jack was found on the Molding Line. The Lead was notified to perform cleaning, as well as ATP and allergen testing, which it passed successfully.

Tempering chart check update 


The temperature remains stable slightly high, but within the permissible range required by the Centerline.
Demolding , metal detector and c6 belts are running correctly
Change of shift for the lot has been verified
Weight, appearance, and texture of the bar are within the specifications

Just to keep everyone informed, Angel Lucero scheduled job 40503 for yesterday and again for today. We verified with Fredy Montalvan the quantity produced during 1st shift today to make sure we had the correct production numbers, since the same job has been running since yesterday. Nathan Gevenois
Capture test has been approved
Capture test
Shift start up checklist
Packaging flow wrap integrity is in good condition. Lot is visible and centered. No air leaks have been observed. We have employees monitored the packaging after the Cavanna
WIP SS is in good conditions and is following the specifications.
We got an alarm on HMI
Demolding , metal detector and c6 belts are running correctly
Update 2nd shift
Water pipe leak . Called maintenance to shut off Glyco. Maintenance is working on it.
Maintenance is currently working to fix the leak
Rose Smuin  Chad Stone Alan Greer  Need to bring to your attention  the group on ML1 came in short handed. Completed the job very professionally.
Packing check. Lot and BB are present and legible on all flow wrappers and display boxes. There are 10 pieces per chocolate in a display box.
Frederick Peairs there’s still 4 employees from 3rd shift here. My team can pass all the accumulation, your team can go home.
We are having issues with the sticker applicator, Nathan Ruiz is here looking at it.
Approximately 2 pieces per mold have this defect the operator and the leader have already been informed and are working on it there is a person taking out these bars
Weight is in the range
Temper of the south side in the range
Thank you for sending them home I will talk to them tonight
Temperature of white and dark chocolate are in the range
Temper of the north  side in the range
Update Appearance The color of the bar meets the standards the bottom of the bar has bubbles
Let's see if we can work with the team on those bubbles. They had it looking really good last night.
Nathan resolved this issue.
Viscosity of dark chocolate
The operator and the supervisor are working on that
Viscosity of white chocolate

Itzel Chavez,

Can you post the countdown for this SKU on the board of where you are starting coming in?

Don’t need anymore viscosity samples. 

Looks good. Thank you!
Yes, working on it.
Countdown
The operator and leader are working on it. TG3

Line keeps running at 18mpm.
No major issues so far with TG’s besides the silver line on TG3 which operators are working on it.
We are trying 19mpm now.
It ran well for 10min at 19mpm and chocolate just jammed on conveyor going to TG3.
We are back to 18mpm.
Update the packaging Meets the standards, readable and centered font and good sealing

Good morning Itzel Chavez, Tyson Fausett

As long as the line is running well, let’s plan on stopping at depositor #1 at 4:30pm. 

If it isn’t running well we can connect about stopping earlier to get ahead on cleaning. 

We should clean Depositor #1 and the Inclusion auger but should not have to clean Depositor #4. 

Let me know if you think of another plan. 👍
Pausing to clean depositor #4 sensors they got chocolate buildup.
White chocolate side on the depositor has a lot of hard chocolate build up on the agitator.
Running.
Update Appearance
Chad Stone is 2nd shift performing capture test so we plan on drain the depositor for them, or do you want just to run straight until 4:30?

Itzel Chavez,
Have we been having to make any large adjustments from the capture test or has it been pretty accurate?
TG5 splitter belt cover safety switch got knocked out of position but is now working again
It’s been accurate.
At 1:10 sticker applicator went down again causing a lot of accumulation on the packaging area, I had to do a 10min pause to clear it out.
Let’s run to 4:30pm

We ran out of v2 of the boxes The spec indicates that it is approved to use the v2 and v3 we see that the deference is very noticeable, when a change is made like this we start another pallet Tyson Fausett Itzel Chavez
Countdown.
After inspecting a pallet of V2 displays, it was determined that the box for the lot is missing, which is why the print is not visible. This pallet will be placed on hold, and Ethan will be notified.
This is the box that’s missing on the display

Angel Cabrera 
Will we have enough displays to complete the order?
Andrea Diaz can you help me look into this please?
Tg4 entrada
Tg3 inicio
Bottom of the bar appearance. Bubbles are appearing consistently, we are waiting as production adjusts this. Will update soon
We had issues with the sticker applicator so we have had to pause the line to get through this before we switch the caddies to V3
We also stopped depositing at 340pm on depositor 1. I had to stop an hour earlier due to not having experienced people on the WDS side to clean the equipment in time.
We are also having silver lining issues on TG4. Operator has been trying to fix it with maintenance. We have tried multiple rolls
All V2 is separated we have started back up using V3 caddies.
Tg5 entrada foto enviada tarde porque apenas se va hacer uso de Ella
Update on the bottom of the bars.
Mixer passed ATP
Molds passed ATP
Is TG there Tyson?
Tyson Fausett, how long until last bar is at the demolder?
No. They weren’t here when I came out to the floor
Yes, we have enough to complete the order .

Last bar just came out of the Gondola. There are a few bins of accumulation we are working through. 
We already started the clean on the inclusion mixer and we will start the clean on Depositor 1 soon.
today I found this tote had been used for scrap. Remind your teams not to use totes for scrap please.
ATPs done so far and have passed
Depositor 1 sliders installed correctly 😁
Salida
Tg3salida
Starting level 2 clean
Adjustments made will report back in 30
We’re replacing it with a new one once ATP is completed.
Sweco screen north side It’s broken
ATP passed
Conveyor belt on TG5 was damaged after clean completed and belts ran. Maintenance is assisting to find cause.
Rose Smuin Chad Stone Rich Brierley Itzel Chavez I have placed tracking sheets on each TG and 4 extra on the table where Richie sits

Rose Smuin Chad Stone Preston Elmer After speaking with maintenance he didn't see anything wrong with the installation of the belt after the cleaning. He noticed 3 initial tears and believe might be a edge in this section caused the issue.

New WO Nathan Topham, Katya Farnsworth, S3 Enrober, William Beattie, Luis Falcon
Thanks! Paula

Maintenance performed an inspection on C21 due to a noise detected by the team leader. It was found that one side was missing the nut that secures the bolt. MM4 was inspected, but it cannot be confirmed whether this part was already missing before the start of the shift
MM3

MM4

Charity Young Mary Montilla, Miriah Manzanares
Enrober SoS

My understanding is that QA is ok for the line to resume production. We are getting the team in place and then we'll start the line. Dakota Adair Curt Willis Victor Mejia Charity Young Miriah Manzanares

Cannot start the line due to high temper. Enrober operator discovered that the return pump was left in "Man", meaning it had drained the sump which we believe to be the cause of the high temper readings. Dakota Adair Curt Willis
Folding the bulk boxes closed is ok? We don't need them taped/sealed shut anymore? Dakota Adair Curt Willis Miriah Manzanares
Start the line
They should be taped.  Please correct this

EEs are reporting to many over 2 pieces together running through the line and getting stuck at the MMs. This is due to having one person by MD. William Beattie is there a chance to have one person more in the other side of MD please?

We don't have any more EEs available. I've been working with the operator in the front to reduce the number of pretzels being fed onto the line which will reduce the number of doubles. Please wait for the changes to reach the back of the line.

Also, EE reported a washer found by MM4 top cover. We don't know where it goes 


Charity Young William Beattie
Thanks for the help
It's an extra that was possibly left over by first shift. It's not one missing from equipment.
Thank you
We're pausing the line while we investigate the found washer or whatever it is. Dakota Adair Curt Willis
I am having the team stopped due a line clearance, they will also be changing to work order and be inspecting a possible source.
Can you make sure IMS properly identifies and tags the last pallet for this order?
It's a nut that was grabbed for as possible replacement and was forgotten by first shift earlier.
In relation to this situation earlier.

It is a very small nut, and there isn't any equipment nearby that uses a nut this size, let alone any that are missing. I believe Caleb York is correct. Is a line clearance and WO change still necessary? Dakota Adair Charity Young Curt Willis
That's 100% what happened. I had thought he had grabbed, but it would appear to not be the case.
This would make the most sense, I don’t believe we will need to but I want to make sure Charity Young, Miriah Manzanares align
Caleb York can you provide the timeline of when the nut was brought onto the line and the details so we can have it documented? I am OK with an email by end of shift.
Lolo will be able to on Monday
Dakota Adair can we add to shift exchange an action to identify parts that are brought onto the line and properly removed from the line and not set somewhere?
I mean some sort of process or something
We will resume production. Dakota Adair Curt Willis
Yes we can we will have an action for this

I have no explanation for the air pressure thing, compressor was running at 115psi. When I made it to the matrixes, the operator was starting it up just fine. 
 
On the x-ray, the operator had logged into it under the Default login and got 3/4 going. I started up 5.

MM3 MM4 MM5 Matrix are clean
Starting heavy wash

Everything is off or night mode

We are experiencing issues with the jar labeling machine, specifically a significant buildup of product. The mechanics and the supervisor are currently on the line working to resolve the fault; we will be taking a brief pause in operations.
Have we tried using labeler B Justin Garner?

Labels are working now. No need to use labeler B.
 
Also, a few things had not been configured on the capper. It is now configured within centerlines and working well
Thank you, Troy!
Passing the accumulated  Total
All accumulation has been passed Justin.  We were able to utilize some people to rotate during lunch in order for us pass accumulation

We are currently having problems with the labels the labels them selves the back plastic rips very easy and is causing the labeler to roll on its self keeps breaking.  Qc will be putting some rolls ok hold
Is any finished product being put on hold?
No FG is being put on hold.  We are at 5 rolls that have been effected due to not thick enough plastic
Thank you for identifying these rolls and keeping the separated!
It takes very little force to snap the plastic.  The backing is very thin
We stop the line for lunch and also to pass all the accumulation
A pallet containing 83 rolls will be put on hold because the label tape tears easily
Belt on CW1 on the jars area was broken, we passing the jars avoid that belt
Isn't broken was out of track due to not running properly, maintenance fixed for now until we finish to run for  being repaired.
Cleaning level 1 started now
The CW belt wasn't installed correctly and had too much slack, causing it to clatter and skip. when the line went down I pulled and re-ran the belt to pull out that extra slack.

Hello Martin Carlos, - Sunny Jagtiani! Just a heads up! We are down to 260 new pallets (40776) in Lindon. 

According to Ethan Bean, we should have a trailer deliver 06/01. Curt Willis -Justin Garner -Tiffanee Taylor-Alan Todd

Daniel Bringas I have talked to Curt Willis already, we are currently 17 people short. Me and my team have structured it to be able to run the lines but we will be running S2 under standard by 2 points so we able to maintain the line.

Curt Willis wanted me to clarify the manning i scheduled my whole team and people training to come in just incase of call offs this is how we are able to run the lines. we also pulled the trash area EE and the wash area EE to help on the line. Further manning reasons will be on the shift report Amy Hadrich Chad Stone.
QA put on hold a total of 83 rolls (125,440 pcs) of item 40797 V4. Daniel Bringas Sunny Jagtiani
Andrea Diaz salt was picked up yesterday by 4 pm.  I have asked on the email chain, where it got delivered to.
It got delivered to L1. We made a warehouse request today to be transferred to L3.
